title: Microsoft-hosted agents update.

Check out the release notes for details.

description: With this update, we've included improvement to strengthen security in Azure DevOps.
summary: With this update, we are including improvements to strengthen security in Azure DevOps. Due to these changes, you may experience and interruption of service and may be asked to re-authenticate after a security event to continue using the service. Check out the release notes for details.

description: With this update, we've added two new extensibility points that you can target in your extensions.

title: New Boards Hub public preview.
description: New Boards Hub is now available in public preview.
summary: New Boards Hubs is now available in public preview. The web platform has been updated to provide a new modern design, responsive reflows, accessibility compliance, and improved page performance. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: New Boards Hub public preview; primarily UI/platform announcement.

title: Query by Parent Id private preview.
description: Query by Parent Id is now available in private preview.
summary: With this update, we included multiple updates to Azure Boards. You can now query work items by Parent Id to get a flat list of children based on parent work items. In addition, we've improved managing work items in a Kanban column withMove to position. This will make it easier to re-order work items in columns that contain a lot of work items. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Boards updates including query by Parent Id and move to position; feature overview without deep configuration.

title: Improved Kanban board user experience.
description: Improved Kanban board user experience (UX).
summary: With this update, we've improved the Kanban board user experience (UX). You can now use the filter to generate a specific search of work items to include in the Kanban board. By using the filter you can generate a more specific search and eliminate the need to re-click theShow more itemsbutton. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Kanban board UX improvements with filtering; no expert-level technical details.

title: Opt-in to receive notifications related to personal access tokens found in a GitHub public repository.
description: Opt-in to receive notifications related to personal access tokens found in a GitHub public repository.
summary: Currently, when we discover a personal access token checked into a GitHub public repository, we send a detailed email notification to the token owner and log an event to your Azure DevOps Organization’s Audit Log. With this update, we've added the option for project collection administrators to opt-in to get notifications when a personal access token for a user that belongs to their organization is found in a GitHub public repository. classification_reason: Opt-in notifications for PATs found in GitHub; security-related but likely just describes an option toggle, not detailed security configuration or RBAC scopes.

title: Filter on work item history.
description: Filter on work item history.
summary: With this update, we included multiple updates to Azure Boards. On Delivery Plans, we added a Last accessed column to the directory page and we improved the experience by allowing you to see all the dependencies lines across all the work items on the screen. In addition, we've added a filter option to the work item history tab. This filter will let you quickly find revisions made to individual fields by specific people. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Boards updates like last accessed column and history filter; UX features, not expert configuration.

title: Azure Pipelines REST API and other security improvements.
description: Whit this update, we improved the Azure Pipelines REST API security.
summary: With this update, we improved the Azure Pipelines REST API security for classic build pipelines. Now, thebuild job authorization scopewill beProject, by default instead ofProject Collection. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Change of default build job authorization scope from Project Collection to Project; security-related but described at a high level without detailed RBAC role/permission mapping.

title: Updates to hosted pipelines images.
description: With this update, we are announcing updates to hosted pipelines images.
summary: With this update, we are announcing updates to hosted pipelines images. The Ubuntu 22.04 image is now generally available, this is the latest version of Ubuntu. In addition, we've included the brownout schedule for Ubuntu 18.04 images. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Hosted image updates and brownout schedule; dates/versions are operational announcements, not configuration or quota references.

title: Addressed multiple requests from the Developer Community.
description: With this update, we addressed multiple requests from the Developer Community.
summary: In response to your feedback, we have prioritized multiple features that you have requested in the Developer Community. In Pipelines, we added support for string split function in YAML expression. In addition, we are now letting you disable showing the last commit message for a pipeline run. In Delivery Plans, we increased the team limit from 15 to 20. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Adds string split function and increases team limit from 15 to 20; while numeric, this is a minor UI/team limit and not a core service limit/timeout in the sense targeted by limits-quotas.

title: Move work items to a column or swimlane from the work item action menu.
description: With this update, move work items to a column or swimlane from the work item action menu.
summary: In this sprint, we've included accessibility improvements to the Kanban board. Now you can move cards to a column or a swimlane from the work items action menu in the Kanban board. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Kanban accessibility improvement for moving work items; UI behavior, not expert technical content.

title: Maintain backlog hierarchy when filtering.
description: With this update, we included multiple updated to Azure Boards.
summary: With this update, we included multiple updates to Azure Boards. In backlogs, we've added the option for you to maintain the hierarchy when filtering. In addition, you can now copy the URL for a work item attachment so you can cut and paste in the description and discussion sections of the work item. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Boards UX updates like maintaining hierarchy when filtering; no expert configuration or limits.

title: Support for template expressions in repository and container resource definitions.
description: With this update, we included support for template expressions in repository and container resource definitions.
summary: With this update, we included support for template expressions in repository and container resource definitions. Now you can use template expressions when defining therefproperty of arepositoryresource in a YAML pipeline to choose the branch of a repository resource. In addition, we've added support for template expressions when defining theendpoint,volumes,ports, andoptionsproperties of acontainerresource in a YAML pipeline. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Support for template expressions in YAML resource definitions; likely shows syntax examples but not configuration tables with defaults/ranges as required.

title: Improvements to strengthen pipelines security.
description: With this update, we are including improvements to strengthen pipelines security in Azure DevOps.
summary: With this update, we are including improvements to strengthen security in Azure DevOps. Now you can use a System-assigned Managed Identity when creating Docker Registry service connections for Azure Container Registry. In addition, we've enhanced access management for agent pools to let you specify the use of resources within a YAML pipeline. Lastly, we are limiting the GitHub access token for forked public GitHub repositories to have read-only scope. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Security improvements including managed identity for ACR service connections and read-only GitHub token scope; summary suggests high-level security changes, not detailed role/permission matrices.

title: Extension updates and performance improvements
description: With this update, we highlight extension updates and performance improvements.
summary:
With this update, we have included extension updates and performance improvements. With an existing ServiceNow integration, we've updated the app to support the Tokyo version of ServiceNow.
We've also made improvements to extension settings to help you manage your installed extensions. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Extension updates and ServiceNow Tokyo support; integration mention is version-level, not parameter-level configuration.

title: Support for subpages table in wiki
description: With this update, we've added support for subpages table in wiki.
summary: You can now add subpages table to your wiki pages so you can see the content and links. In Boards, you can now add colors to swimlane and lock custom fields from being edited. We also continue our investments in security and added a new PAT scope for managing pipeline authorization and approvals and checks. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Wiki subpages table and new PAT scope; PAT scope mention is security-related but likely just a name, without full RBAC/permission mapping.

title: Improved pipeline security and administrator control
description: With this update, we improved pipelines security resources and administrator control.
summary: With this update, we improved pipelines security resources critical to building and deploying your applications. In response to your feedback, we've also made the Copy Dashboard feature easily accessible. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Improved pipeline security resources and admin control; summary does not indicate specific RBAC roles, parameters, or limits.

title: Pull request widget for multiple repos is now in private preview
description: With this update, we are excited to announce that you can select multiple repositories in the pull request widget.
summary: The pull request widget lets you display active pull requests (PRs) requested by your team. Previously, the pull request widget let you select one repository to show in your dashboard. With this update, we are excited to announce that you can select multiple repositories in the pull request widget. In Pipelines, we've made it easier to find stages using the stages side panel. Now you can filter for stages based on their status. Check out the release notes for details. Search for stages in side p
classification_reason: Pull request widget multi-repo support and stage search; feature overview without expert-level technical details.

title: Improved dashboard management and pipeline security
description: With this update, we have enable the capability to view when a dashboard was last visited or modified providing you with view to determine which ones can be removed.
summary: With this update, we have added the capability to track when a dashboard was last viewed or modified. This improvement will help project administrators evaluate if a dashboard needs to be removed due to inactivity, further preventing reaching stored dashboard thresholds. We also continue our investments in security. Now, OAuth2 is the default authentication mechanism when creating a GitHub Enterprise Server hosted pipeline. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Dashboard last-visited tracking and OAuth2 default for GHES pipelines; security note is a default choice, not a detailed configuration reference.

title: Pipeline agent updates and Azure Boards private preview opportunities

classification_reason: Describes authentication changes and managed DevOps pools GA; summary does not indicate detailed configuration matrices or limits.

This includes ending support for new OAuth app registrations starting April 2025, though existing apps will continue working until full retirement in 2026. description: Expanded security scanning and GitHub pull request integration with Azure Boards

Check out the release notes for details.

classification_reason: Describes new capabilities (YAML in Test Plans, notebook diffing); summary does not show detailed configuration or error mappings.

description: GitHub Secret Protection and GitHub Code Security available as standalone products in Azure DevOps
summary: You can now get GitHub Secret Protection and GitHub Code Security as standalone products in Azure DevOps. Secret Protection provides access to secret scanning, push protection, and security overview experiences. Code Security provides access to all dependency scanning, code scanning, and security overview experiences. classification_reason: Release note summarizing product availability and a Test Plans directory; not a structured technical reference.

title: Azure DevOps MCP Server in public preview
description: Azure DevOps MCP Server in public preview
summary: We’re thrilled to introduce the public preview of the Azure DevOps MCP Server! You can now access Azure DevOps data such as work items, pull requests, builds, test plans, and more thru a self-hosted, on-premises solution from GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code. This integration enhances Copilot’s ability to provide intelligent, context-aware suggestions while ensuring your data remains securely within your environment. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Public preview announcement for MCP Server; summary is high-level and does not expose detailed configuration or limits.

title: New Boards Hub enabled for all customers across all regions
description: New Boards Hub enabled for all customers across all regions
summary: In this sprint, we're excited to announce that the New Boards Hub is enabled for all customers globally! Try out the New Boards Hub and share your feedback to help shape what's next. You can find details about how to share your feedback in the release notes. In addition, the Markdown support for comments is generally available with this release. This has been a long-awaited feature requested in the Developer Community. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Announcement of New Boards Hub and markdown support; no indication of deep technical reference content.

title: Azure Boards and Azure Pipelines integration with GitHub Enterprise Cloud
description: Azure Boards and Azure Pipelines integration with GitHub Enterprise Cloud
summary: Azure Boards and Azure Pipelines now support integration with GitHub Enterprise Cloud organizations with data residency enabled. You can learn more about how to create a new connection for Azure Boards and a new pipeline from the release notes. In addition, with this release we are no longer allowing organizations to create public projects. This change won't impact organizations that already have the Allow public policy enabled. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Release note about integration support and policy change; summary lacks detailed technical matrices or configuration parameters.

title: Newly generated OAuth client secrets will be shown only at time of creation
description: Newly generated OAuth client secrets will be shown only at time of creation
summary: With this release we are changing how Azure DevOps displays OAuth client secrets. Now, newly generated client secrets will be shown only once at the time of creation. This means that they will no longer be retrievable via the UI or API. This change will reduce the risk of accidental exposure and encourages secure storage practices. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Describes a change in OAuth secret visibility; likely policy/behavioral note rather than a detailed configuration or troubleshooting guide.

This feature will let you create work items, add detailed instructions in the description, and seamlessly send them to Copilot. classification_reason: Private preview announcement; mostly conceptual description of capability without detailed configuration reference.

title: Secret expiration detection in Azure service connection
description: Secret expiration detection in Azure service connection
summary: Service connections that use a service principal with a secret have always supported secret rotation via connection updates. However, this capability was not clearly surfaced in the configuration experience. We've now enhanced the Azure service connection workflow to automatically detect when a secret is nearing expiration and prompt users to rotate it, streamlining credential management and reducing the risk of service disruptions. Check out the release notes for details.
classification_reason: Explains new secret expiration detection behavior; summary does not show detailed parameter tables or error-code mappings.
